prompt_text:
You are shown a SKELETON view of a python repository: class headers,
inheritance, method signatures, and attributes — but NO method bodies.

Use this signature-level view to detect structural violations of Liskov Substitution Principle (LSP):
- Fat interfaces / bloated classes (many unrelated method groups)
- Clients forced to implement methods they don't need (abstract methods / NotImplementedError / empty overrides)
- Inheritance hierarchies where subclasses alter the contract shape
- Broad AP...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "requests/src/requests/cookies.py",
      "entity_name": "RequestsCookieJar",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 176,
      "line_end": 437,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Incompatible multiple inheritance from cookielib.CookieJar and MutableMapping",
      "reasoning": "RequestsCookieJar inherits from both cookielib.CookieJar and MutableMapping. The __iter__ method contract for CookieJar (standard library) requires yielding Cookie objects, while the MutableMapping contract (collections.abc) requires yielding keys (strings). Since a single __iter__ implementation cannot satisfy both behavioral contracts simultaneously, the class cannot be safely substituted for both of its base types in all contexts."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "requests/src/requests/auth.py",
      "entity_name": "HTTPProxyAuth",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 99,
      "line_end": 104,
      "principle": "LSP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Subclass violates the behavioral postcondition of the parent class by altering header targets",
      "reasoning": "HTTPProxyAuth inherits from HTTPBasicAuth but overrides the __call__ method to set 'Proxy-Authorization' instead of the 'Authorization' header. This violates the postcondition of the parent class (which is to provide authentication for the origin server). A client expecting an HTTPBasicAuth object to authorize a request will find HTTPProxyAuth non-substitutable because it fails to authenticate the request to the target resource."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: Incompatible multiple inheritance from cookielib.CookieJar and MutableMapping
findings.reasoning: RequestsCookieJar inherits from both cookielib.CookieJar and MutableMapping. The __iter__ method contract for CookieJar (standard library) requires yielding Cookie objects, while the MutableMapping contract (collections.abc) requires yielding keys (strings). Since a single __iter__ implementation cannot satisfy both behavioral contracts simultaneously, the class cannot be safely substituted for both of its base types in all contexts.

findings.description: Subclass violates the behavioral postcondition of the parent class by altering header targets
findings.reasoning: HTTPProxyAuth inherits from HTTPBasicAuth but overrides the __call__ method to set 'Proxy-Authorization' instead of the 'Authorization' header. This violates the postcondition of the parent class (which is to provide authentication for the origin server). A client expecting an HTTPBasicAuth object to authorize a request will find HTTPProxyAuth non-substitutable because it fails to authenticate the request to the target resource.
